required_amendments.phase: Phase 6 (F4 / TC-3 command_stop) -- daemon command waiter idempotency
required_amendments.amendment: Add an explicit terminal-state skip guard to the COMMAND lifecycle waiter and make command_stop's kill path race-safe. The plan's claim (lines 24, 246) that command_stop can 'coordinate with the lifecycle waiter via the existing terminal-state idempotency (PTY checked-then-skipped pattern)' is FALSE for the command path: that pattern exists ONLY in the PTY waiter (pty_command.rs:391-400), not the command waiter, and jobs.cancel()/finish() are themselves non-idempotent (unconditional state set + Some(draft) every call). As written, command_stop calling jobs.cancel(job_id) synchronously while the command waiter ALSO calls jobs.cancel/finish + bucket_append produces TWO lifecycle drafts (doubled bucket event + doubled audit row), and the Phase 6 test asserting 'exactly one cancel/command_exited event' (line 256) will FAIL. Required: (1) In crates/daemon/src/command.rs, before the waiter builds the draft and calls finish/cancel/bucket_append (~command.rs:667-680), add a guard mirroring pty_command.rs:391-400 -- if waiter_jobs.get(job_id) reports state already Exited/Failed/Cancelled, update metrics only and return without re-appending. (2) Add crates/daemon/src/command.rs to Phase 6's change list FOR THE WAITER GUARD (currently it is listed only for the cancel-handle field at line 246). (3) Define authoritative terminal-state ownership: stop() sets Cancelled only if the job is not already terminal (check-then-set under the live/jobs write lock); a single critical section decides stop-vs-natural-exit so a job that self-exits microseconds before the kill is not mislabeled. (4) Make the integration test (line 256) tolerant for self-exit races: assert terminal AND (Cancelled OR Exited) when stopping a may-self-exit job, and assert STRICT Cancelled only for a guaranteed-long-running job (e.g. sleep 60). Document that command_stop on an already-terminal job is a no-op returning the existing terminal state, not an error.
evidence: crates/core/src/job.rs:154-184 (finish: g.get_mut(&job_id)? then unconditional rec.state set + Some(draft), NO terminal guard)
crates/core/src/job.rs:187-207 (cancel: same, NO terminal guard)
crates/daemon/src/command.rs:668-680 (command waiter unconditionally calls finish/cancel then bucket_append, NO skip-on-terminal guard)
crates/daemon/src/pty_command.rs:391-400 (PTY waiter DOES guard: returns early if state is Exited/Failed/Cancelled)
plan-final.json:24, plan-final.json:246, plan-final.json:256 (claims PTY idempotency precedent applies + tests exactly-one-event)
source_findings: adversarial-breaker blocker: command_stop double-emit / no terminal guard in command waiter
adversarial-breaker major: stop() oneshot-fire vs waiter outcome-classification race

required_amendments.phase: Phase 5 (F5 / TC-5 self_check spawn round-trip) -- --selfcheck-noop CLI shape
required_amendments.amendment: Replace the 'hidden --selfcheck-noop mode/flag' with a real HIDDEN clap SUBCOMMAND, and spawn it as a valid subcommand argv. The daemon binary's Cli is #[derive(Parser)] with a REQUIRED #[command(subcommand)] cmd: Cmd over a CLOSED set {Check, Start, PrintConfig, Update} (main.rs:28-75); Cli::parse() (main.rs:87) runs before any dispatch. Spawning current_exe() with a bare '--selfcheck-noop' flag -> clap errors on the unknown arg AND on the missing required subcommand -> child exits 2 on a HEALTHY daemon -> Phase 5 sets failures>0 -> self_check false-REDs, directly violating the plan's own invariant 'TC-5 self_check NEVER false-REDs a healthy daemon' (line 303) and the F5 'NEVER false-RED' promise (line 30). On Windows it is worse: windows_subsystem=windows (main.rs:4) means no console for the clap stderr, but it still exits nonzero. Required: (1) Add Cmd::SelfcheckNoop (with #[command(hide = true)]) to the Cmd enum in crates/daemon/src/main.rs, handled at the top of main() to return ExitCode::SUCCESS BEFORE any further work; ignore all other argv/env (leaf mode). (2) Spawn current_exe() with argv [exe, 'selfcheck-noop'] (a valid subcommand), NOT '--selfcheck-noop'. (3) Re-describe the Phase 5 main.rs change item (line 216) and the F5 decision (line 30) accordingly; reconcile the main.rs module-doc/long_about (main.rs:11-18, 36-37 'Does NOT spawn child commands by itself') with the new internal self-probe target. (4) Add a positive test asserting 'terminal-commanderd selfcheck-noop' exits 0 so a future clap refactor that breaks the subcommand is caught instead of silently false-REDing the whole daemon.
evidence: crates/daemon/src/main.rs:28-48 (clap Parser, REQUIRED cmd: Cmd subcommand, not Option)
crates/daemon/src/main.rs:50-75 (closed Cmd enum: Check/Start/PrintConfig/Update -- no noop)
crates/daemon/src/main.rs:86-87 (Cli::parse() before dispatch; errors exit nonzero)
crates/daemon/src/main.rs:4 (#![cfg_attr(windows, windows_subsystem="windows")] -- no console)
plan-final.json:30, plan-final.json:216, plan-final.json:303 (proposes --selfcheck-noop flag; promises never-false-RED)
source_findings: adversarial-breaker blocker: current_exe() --selfcheck-noop rejected by clap -> false-RED
feasibility-engineer note: --selfcheck-noop must be a hidden clap subcommand, not raw flag
scope-guardian minor: new CLI surface under-documented
policy-security-reviewer minor: --selfcheck-noop must be an inert leaf mode

required_amendments.phase: Phase 4 (F6 / TC-4 argv_head) -- real argv redaction
required_amendments.amendment: Build a REAL argv redactor before Phase 4 lands; do NOT rely on format_argv_metadata for secret redaction. The plan's central TC-4 safety claim -- that argv_head is 'routed through the existing format_argv_metadata-style redaction' (lines 36, 178, 199, 301) -- is FALSE: format_argv_metadata (command.rs:989-1004) only TRUNCATES each argv element to 128 bytes and serializes {"argv":[...]} verbatim; it redacts nothing. A repo-wide search confirms the ONLY redaction in the tree is per-rule capture redaction on command OUTPUT lines (sifters/src/lib.rs:309-343), never on argv. Because argv_head is argv[0..2] (program + first two tokens), bounding the head does NOT bound away secrets -- it keeps exactly the window where secrets sit ('psql postgres://user:pass@host', 'mysql -ppassword', 'curl -H Authorization: Bearer <tok>'), all under 128 bytes so truncation never trips. Required: either (a) add a dedicated argv redactor that masks values following secret-shaped flags (-H/--header, -p/--password, --token, --secret, Authorization:, and key=value where key matches *_TOKEN/*_SECRET/*_PASSWORD/*_KEY) and replaces the value with <redacted>; OR (b) surface ONLY argv[0] (program basename) plus tag and drop argv[1..2]. Update every plan section that asserts format_argv_metadata redacts to say 'format_argv_metadata only truncates; a NEW argv redactor is required.' Make the Phase 4 redaction test (line 189) assert against a real secret pattern (Authorization header, password flag), not just length -- as written that test would FAIL against the current helper, which is the tell.
evidence: crates/daemon/src/command.rs:989-1004 (format_argv_metadata: per-item 128-byte truncate, NO redaction)
crates/sifters/src/lib.rs:309-343 (the ONLY redaction in tree: per-rule capture redaction on output lines)
Grep redact|scrub|sanitize|mask across crates: zero argv redactor
plan-final.json:36, plan-final.json:178, plan-final.json:199, plan-final.json:301 (all claim format_argv_metadata redaction)
source_findings: policy-security-reviewer blocker: argv_head is NOT redacted, leaks secrets in argv[0..2]

required_amendments.phase: Phase 1 (F1 / is_idempotent classification) -- SubscriptionPull + omitted variants
required_amendments.amendment: Fix two classification errors in is_idempotent(). (1) RECLASSIFY SubscriptionPull as NON-idempotent (false), NOT idempotent as the plan states at line 82. Unlike BucketWait (client-cursor-driven and fully replayable -- the client passes cursor and the daemon returns events > cursor, confirmed bucket.rs:54-65), SubscriptionPull advances per-consumer offsets and COMMITS them server-side INSIDE the pull (pull.rs:541 advance, pull.rs:544 commit) BEFORE serializing the response. A lost-then-retried pull (the exact transport-failure case the retry-gate fires on) restarts from the already-advanced offset and the previously-drained events are gone forever, converting the documented 'lossless pull' (protocol.rs:289) into a lossy one on the retry path the gate preserves. Add a unit assertion that SubscriptionPull is_idempotent()==false with a comment citing the server-side offset commit. (2) COMPLETE the enumeration with the governing RULE stated explicitly at the top of the helper: 'return false for any RPC whose retry could create/duplicate a server-side resource or mint a fresh id; return true only for pure bounded reads and idempotent-effect repositioning.' Then classify the variants the plan omits entirely: SubscriptionOpen=false (mints a fresh sub_id + registry slot -- a blind retry LEAKS a slot and can trip SubscriptionLimitExceeded), SubscriptionClose=false (frees a slot; conservative non-retry), AuditSince=true (pure read), CommandOutputTail=true (pure read). Add these to the Phase 1 table-driven exhaustiveness test, including a case asserting SubscriptionOpen-retry would leak a slot.
evidence: crates/daemon/src/subscriptions/pull.rs:541,544 (offsets advanced in place then commit() server-side during the pull, before response)
crates/ipc/src/protocol.rs:289-294 (SubscriptionPull = 'Multiplexed, lossless pull')
crates/daemon/src/ipc/handlers/bucket.rs:54-65 (BucketWait IS client-cursor-driven via params.cursor -- the safe contrast)
crates/ipc/src/protocol.rs:283-302 (SubscriptionOpen mints fresh sub_id; SubscriptionClose frees slot; AuditSince read; all omitted from plan:82)
plan-final.json:82 (classifies SubscriptionPull as true; omits Open/Close/AuditSince/CommandOutputTail)
source_findings: adversarial-breaker major: SubscriptionPull idempotent=true makes the subscription lossy under auto-retry
scope-guardian major: is_idempotent omits SubscriptionOpen/Close (slot leak) and AuditSince
coherence-auditor major: is_idempotent omits SubscriptionOpen/Close/AuditSince; SubscriptionOpen retry double-effect

required_amendments.phase: Phase 5 (F5 / dispatch_envelope await propagation) -- delete the phantom edit
required_amendments.amendment: DELETE the 'dispatch_envelope / alternate Windows named-pipe dispatch path -- ALSO gains .await for handle_self_check' change item (line 214), the corresponding risk line (line 234), and the 'BOTH dispatch sites' framing in the F5 decision (lines 30-31) and posture (line 2). This 'judge graft' corrects a NON-EXISTENT second call site. handle_self_check has exactly ONE caller: dispatch() at server.rs:541. dispatch_envelope (server.rs:821-828) is a one-line delegate -- its entire body is `dispatch(state, boot, req_env, peer).await` -- with NO SelfCheck arm of its own; the Windows named-pipe server calls dispatch_envelope, which already awaits the (already-async) dispatch. So making handle_self_check async requires adding `.await` at EXACTLY ONE place (server.rs:541) and ZERO changes to dispatch_envelope/pipe_server. Replace the deleted item with: 'handle_self_check has a single call site (server.rs:541); add .await there. dispatch_envelope and the named-pipe server reach SelfCheck transitively through dispatch and need no edit -- verify the Windows build compiles, but expect zero source change in pipe_server.rs/dispatch_envelope.' This removes a load-bearing 'graft' that the plan congratulates itself on four times but which rests on a misread of the delegation chain, and prevents an implementer hunting for a phantom second arm.
evidence: crates/daemon/src/ipc/server.rs:540-543 (the SOLE handle_self_check call, inside dispatch)
crates/daemon/src/ipc/server.rs:821-828 (dispatch_envelope body = dispatch(...).await, no SelfCheck arm)
SymForge/grep: only one handle_self_check reference in server.rs (server.rs:541)
plan-final.json:2, plan-final.json:30-31, plan-final.json:214, plan-final.json:234 (claims BOTH dispatch sites need .await)
source_findings: feasibility-engineer major: dispatch_envelope judge-graft fixes a phantom edit
scope-guardian major: Phase 5 invents a non-existent second dispatch site
coherence-auditor major: judge graft corrects a non-existent second call site
adversarial-breaker minor: same -- only one await site, not two

required_amendments.amendment: Reconcile Phase 6's estimated_files with the plan's own '<=10 files per phase' invariant (lines 2, 42, 295). The Phase 6 change list enumerates ~16-17 EDITED files: process.rs, command.rs, protocol.rs, ipc/lib.rs (re-export), ipc/mod.rs (re-export), daemon/lib.rs (re-export), server.rs, tools.rs, mcp_stdio.rs, mcp_live_daemon.rs, daemon_unavailable_envelope.rs, mcp-tool-fixture-map.v1.json, command_stop.v1.json (new), system_discover.v1.json, TOOL_CONTROL_SURFACE.md, SPEC.md, RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md -- the three re-export sites are confirmed real (ipc/lib.rs:57, daemon ipc/mod.rs:62, daemon lib.rs:65). Even excluding the 3 docs the code+test+fixture set is ~13-14, so the declared estimated_files:10 undercounts by ~60% and the phase cannot be a single <=10-file PR. Required: either (a) correct estimated_files to the true count (~16) and explicitly EXEMPT Phase 6 from the <=10 rule in the invariant, documenting that the atomic count-anchor set (5 name lists + 3 count assertions + fixture map + system_discover fixture + minimal_tool_args, all of which MUST land in one commit or CI goes red both directions) forces a single larger atomic PR; OR (b) split Phase 6 into 6a (daemon kill capability + IPC method + re-exports + dispatch, behind no MCP tool yet) and 6b (MCP tool surface + fixtures + docs), verifying the parity test tolerates a DISCOVERABLE_METHODS entry not yet in the MCP catalogue (it compares method_name vs DISCOVERABLE_METHODS, not vs the MCP tool catalogue).
evidence: plan-final.json:2, plan-final.json:42, plan-final.json:295 (invariant: each phase <=10 files)
plan-final.json:245-253 (Phase 6 change list enumerating ~16-17 files)
plan-final.json:278 (estimated_files:10)
crates/ipc/src/lib.rs:57, crates/daemon/src/ipc/mod.rs:62, crates/daemon/src/lib.rs:65 (PtyCommandStop re-export sites confirmed -> CommandStop needs all three)
source_findings: coherence-auditor blocker: Phase 6 violates the <=10 files invariant; estimated_files:10 wrong

required_amendments.amendment: Add the missing CommandStartRequest + handler plumbing so dedup_nonce actually reaches start_combed; as scoped the field is silently dropped. Phase 2 (lines 113-116) adds dedup_nonce to the WIRE struct CommandStartParams and adds the dedup check inside start_combed, but start_combed does NOT receive CommandStartParams -- it receives the daemon-internal CommandStartRequest, and the IPC handler handle_command_start_combed hand-builds CommandStartRequest field-by-field, so an unlisted dedup_nonce is dropped at that conversion (the exact 'compiles + passes tests but silently drops the field' fake-success class the plan elsewhere guards against). Required: add to Phase 2's change list (a) add dedup_nonce: Option<String> to CommandStartRequest in crates/daemon/src/command.rs, and (b) thread dedup_nonce: params.dedup_nonce.clone() in handle_command_start_combed (crates/daemon/src/ipc/handlers/command.rs). Bump Phase 2 estimated_files from 4 to 5 and add a round-trip test asserting a nonce sent over IPC is OBSERVED by start_combed's dedup path (not merely that CommandStartParams decodes it). Also, for the in-flight map itself, drop the 'behind the existing live lock' option (line 113): the live lock is Arc<RwLock<HashMap<JobId, JobBinding>>> with a different key/value type, so the dedup map must be a NEW Arc<Mutex<HashMap<u64,(JobId,BucketId,Instant)>>> field on CommandRuntime, cloned into the lifecycle waiter closure (let waiter_dedup = Arc::clone(&self.dedup);) alongside waiter_live so eviction can run on the completion paths.
evidence: crates/daemon/src/command.rs:572-581 (start_combed builds JobConfig from req: CommandStartRequest, the internal struct)
crates/daemon/src/command.rs:584-592 (JobBinding insert; start_combed operates on internal types, not CommandStartParams)
crates/daemon/src/command.rs:610 (waiter_live = Arc::clone(&self.live) -- the established capture pattern any new dedup map must mirror)
plan-final.json:113-116 (Phase 2 change list adds nonce to CommandStartParams + start_combed only; never names CommandStartRequest or the handler)
source_findings: scope-guardian major: Phase 2 omits CommandStartRequest + handle_command_start_combed plumbing for dedup_nonce
feasibility-engineer minor: 'behind the existing live lock' is infeasible; needs a new Arc field captured into the waiter

required_amendments.phase: Phase 6 (F4 / TC-3) -- audit-before-kill ordering on the deny path
required_amendments.amendment: Specify the exact ordering of policy-deny, job-existence check, and audit-row emission in CommandRuntime::stop so a denied caller cannot use it as a job-existence oracle and no job_id is written to a locally-readable audit row on the deny path. The plan (line 246) says 'evaluate PolicyAction::CommandSignal ... emit the audit row BEFORE the kill' but leaves the deny ordering unspecified, and the cited PTY precedent does the opposite of what a deny path needs (PTY checks existence FIRST and audits the job_id only AFTER the job is confirmed and killed, and PTY is not policy-gated at all). Required ordering: (1) evaluate PolicyAction::CommandSignal FIRST; on Deny, emit a deny audit row whose SUBJECT is the peer identity (identity_audit_subject), NOT the job_id, and return PolicyDenied WITHOUT touching the live map -- so a denied caller learns nothing about whether the job exists and no job_id leaks into a sibling-readable audit row; (2) only AFTER an Allow verdict, do the live-map lookup (UnknownJob if absent); (3) emit the job-id-bearing 'allow' audit BEFORE firing the cancel handle. Tighten the Phase 6 deny test (line 257): assert the deny audit subject is the peer and does NOT contain the job_id wire string, and that stop() returns the SAME error for a live job_id and a bogus job_id under read_only_observer (no existence oracle).
evidence: crates/daemon/src/pty_command.rs:391-400 (PTY checks terminal/existence first; PTY stop is NOT policy-gated -- not a deny-path precedent)
crates/daemon/src/command.rs:701-708 (command audit rows embed job_id.to_wire_string() into the subject -- locally readable via audit_since)
plan-final.json:246 (evaluate policy + 'audit row BEFORE the kill' with no deny/existence ordering)
plan-final.json:257 (deny test exists but does not constrain the audit subject or the existence-oracle)
source_findings: policy-security-reviewer major: audit-before-kill ordering under-specified; existence-oracle + audit job_id disclosure on deny path

optional_improvements: Phase 2 / cross-cutting (peer-scoped dedup): Restrict the argv+cwd+tag fallback to collapse ONLY requests from the SAME peer identity -- key the dedup map on (peer_uid/peer_sid, argv, cwd, tag), not (argv, cwd, tag) alone. dispatch() already has PeerIdentity in scope (server.rs:825 signature). job_id/bucket_id are UUIDv7 (not guessable), but the low-entropy argv+cwd+tag fallback would let a sibling local client that guesses another client's about-to-run command ('cargo build' in a known cwd, no tag) submit the identical start within ~3s and receive the SAME (job_id,bucket_id) -- a cross-client live-handle disclosure it could then command_stop or subscribe to. Peer-scoping eliminates this while preserving same-peer retry dedup. If threading peer identity is too invasive, drop the argv+cwd+tag fallback and dedup ONLY on an explicit client nonce (which a client only knows for its own request). [policy-security-reviewer major]

Phase 1 (split self-heal from re-send): try_self_heal() (the Health re-probe / cached-status flip) is currently bundled INSIDE the gated retry branch (daemon_client.rs:196), so gating the whole branch on is_idempotent() also skips the status self-heal for mutating RPCs -- a mutating call immediately after a legitimate daemon replace fails once even when the daemon is already back. Move try_self_heal() out of the gated branch: call it on a transport error for BOTH idempotent and mutating RPCs, but only RE-SEND when is_idempotent(). Restores transparent reconnect-status recovery for mutating RPCs without re-sending them. [adversarial-breaker note]

Phase 3 (degraded state honesty): In the degraded result, mark state as UNKNOWN/last-observed rather than silently defaulting to JobState::Running (tools.rs:651). The agent must not read 'Running' as 'confirmed still running' when the daemon may have died. Also have recover_hint tell the agent to FIRST confirm daemon liveness (health) before polling command_status in a loop, so a permanently-dead daemon is detected rather than polled forever (otherwise: degraded says 'poll', poll transport-fails, loop). Keeps F2's job_id preservation without over-promising recoverability. [adversarial-breaker major]

Phase 3 (final non-blocking drain on deadline-exit): After the wall-clock while loop exits due to deadline while NON-terminal, do ONE final non-blocking BucketWait (timeout_ms: Some(0)) to drain events that arrived since the last cursor before building the degraded result, mirroring the existing terminal short-circuit drain (tools.rs:683). Document in the run_and_watch contract that on wait_exhausted, signals is best-effort and cursor is authoritative for resumption. [adversarial-breaker minor]

Phase 6 (JobBinding Clone derive): JobBinding derives Clone (command.rs:217); oneshot::Sender is not Clone, so adding cancel: Option<oneshot::Sender<()>> breaks the derive. State explicitly in the plan: remove the Clone derive (verified JobBinding is never whole-cloned -- rebind and live_jobs read individual fields), keep Debug. Pre-empts an implementer reaching for the Arc<Mutex<Option<...>>> pattern the plan explicitly says to avoid (line 272). [coherence-auditor minor]

Phase 6 (probe mutability + anchor refresh): Change 'let probe = match ProcessProbe::spawn(...)' (command.rs:547) to 'let mut probe = ...' so take_cancel_handle(&mut self) is callable; take the handle immediately after the spawn success arm and store it in the JobBinding inserted at command.rs:584. Re-anchor Phase 6: id mint at command.rs:474 (not :472), spawn-failure return at :551-561 (not :548-560), JobBinding insert at :584 (not ~:589), drive_to_exit move at :620 (not ~:606), eviction at the cancel/finish branch ~:668-671 (not ~:687, which is the second metrics write). [feasibility-engineer minor]

Phase 1 (operation-neutral mutating remedy): The single mutating-failure remedy text 'the command MAY have started; call command_status/runtime_state to confirm' is start-specific and reads as nonsense on a STOP/Shutdown transport failure. Make it operation-neutral: 'this mutating operation may or may not have taken effect; call command_status/runtime_state to confirm the actual state before re-issuing.' Honest for start, stop, and shutdown alike. [policy-security-reviewer minor]

Phase 1 (correct the stale doc comment): When fixing transport_unavailable_error, also correct the now-stale doc comment at tools.rs:1790-1796 which claims the envelope is 'never a raw internal_error (-32603)' -- the very falsehood the TC-1a sub-defect is about. Name the comment fix explicitly so a documented lie is not left in place. [scope-guardian note]

Phase 2 (resolve the load-bearing dedup mechanism): The plan makes dedup_nonce optional ('may optionally set', line 116) so in production run_and_watch/command_start_combed send None and the operative key is always the argv+cwd+tag window -- but the never-collapse invariant (line 302) and the never-collapse test (line 120) both rest on DISTINCT nonces, a path production never exercises. Either make the adapter ALWAYS generate a per-call nonce (so a genuine LLM re-issue that copies nothing gets a new nonce => distinct jobs), or state explicitly that the fallback window is the real mechanism, quantify it precisely, and add a never-collapse test for the NO-NONCE identical-signature case. Note Phase 1 already removes the auto-retry, so define exactly which caller reuses a nonce or the guard collapses nothing. [coherence-auditor major + scope-guardian minor]

Phase 3 (cursor superset coherence): The plan calls the degraded result a 'strict superset' built by 'one shared builder' (lines 12-13), but the degraded shape adds `cursor` while the normal-path change (line 147) adds only degraded:false + recover_hint:null, NOT cursor -- so it is not a strict superset and there are effectively two builders. Either add `cursor` to the NORMAL payload too (genuinely one builder / superset, low risk since R3 verified no schema test gates run_and_watch result fields) or drop the 'strict superset'/'one shared builder' framing and describe the two field sets accurately. [coherence-auditor major]

Phase 0 (RISK_REGISTER widening row): Add a RISK_REGISTER row recording that argv_head/tag in ProbeListEntry surfaces the program + bounded redacted head of every live job to ANY local IPC client (read handlers take no peer authz today), accepted under the single-tenant-local-daemon trust model and mitigated by the new argv redactor; cross-link R-06. Makes the threat-model widening (from R-06's per-job-client to any-local-client) an informed acceptance rather than a silent scope change. [policy-security-reviewer note]

Phase 6 (stale module-doc count): The daemon_unavailable_envelope.rs module doc (line 12) says 'exercises all 30 daemon-backed tools' -- a SECOND stale count in the very file Phase 6 edits, not in the plan's 'all VERIFIED' anchor set. It is a //! doc comment (won't fail CI) but should be reconciled to the real daemon-backed count when the assertion is bumped, and the count set re-labeled 'gated + known prose' rather than 'all'. [feasibility-engineer minor]

Phase 4 (drop the always-None path lift): For the Command arm, '(+ path)' from BucketSource is a guaranteed no-op -- start_combed records path: None for command buckets (command.rs:509). Drop '(+ path)' for the Command arm or note it as future-proofing; keep tag-lifting, which is the real win (tag IS recorded at command.rs:510). Also reword the TC-4 invariant from 'so fields are not silently dropped' to 'so the CLI table surfaces the new identity columns (wire fields round-trip regardless; this is CLI display completeness, not data integrity)' -- probe_rows is the single shared render path (render.rs:164) covering jobs/probes/runtime-state. [feasibility-engineer note + scope-guardian minor]

Cross-cutting (MCP guard 2 substring trap): MCP guard 2 (scripts/linux-gate.sh ~57-60) is a bare grep for tokio::fs/std::fs/File::open/read_to_string/read_to_end over crates/mcp/src with NO doc-comment exemption (unlike guard 1). Add a one-line caution to the guards invariant: any new comment/hint/string literal added to crates/mcp/src in Phases 1/2/3/6 must avoid those exact literals (write 'file system' not 'std::fs'). No design change, just a non-obvious CI trap to flag. [coherence-auditor minor]

Phase 1 (lock in PTY-path coverage): Add a Phase 1 integration test asserting pty_command_start (not only run_and_watch) produces exactly ONE PTY job under an induced transport timeout. PtyCommandStart is already in the is_idempotent=false set so the gate covers it, but the coverage is currently only implied via the table; an explicit test locks it. [scope-guardian note]
rejected_findings: REJECTED -- policy-security-reviewer minor 'self_check noop could be abused as arbitrary-exec / must not bypass policy gates': This is a confirmation, not a defect against the plan. The self_check handler hardcodes the argv (the caller supplies none), and the plan already routes the spawn through the normal CommandRuntime path so it inherits validate_argv + the shell guard + policy.evaluate (command.rs:431-457 region). The reviewer's own evidence shows the plan implies the safe path. The substantive part (a hidden inert leaf mode that exits 0 before any arg/env/socket work) is already folded into the required Phase 5 CLI-shape amendment. No separate plan change required beyond stating the no-bypass + leaf-mode invariant, which the Phase 5 amendment covers.

REJECTED as a finding (it is positive confirmation, no amendment) -- scope-guardian notes 'Phase 1 DOES fix pty_command_start + command_start_combed' and 'transport_unavailable_error/-32603/into_mcp_error are exactly as claimed': both are explicit VERIFIED confirmations that the plan's premises hold; the reviewer assigns no amendment. I independently confirmed daemon_client.rs:191-198 is the shared retry path and tools.rs:651/665/703/683 match the plan. Recorded as sound, not actionable. (The doc-comment-fix sub-suggestion is carried as an optional improvement.)

REJECTED as already-handled -- adversarial-breaker note 'F1 retry-gate removes reconnect self-heal for mutating RPCs; acceptable trade': The reviewer's own verdict is that this is the CORRECT safety posture (fail-closed beats silent double-act), so it is not a defect in the plan's decision.
